title: Phyllanthus nemoralis
intro: Phyllanthus nemoralis is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as a type of Phyllanthus shrub. The health benefits of Phyllanthus nemoralis include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, as well as its ability to support liver and kidney health. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus nemoralis include flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Phyllanthus nemoralis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including liver and kidney disease, as well as inflammation and oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus nemoralis belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as the Phyllanthus family or the leafy greens family. This family is a part of the larger order Malpighiales in the clade rosidae. The family Phyllanthaceae is made up of around 60 genera and over 1,000 species of flowering plants. Phyllanthus nemoralis is a member of the genus Phyllanthus, which is one of the largest genera in the family Phyllanthaceae. The classification of Phyllanthus nemoralis is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that have confirmed its relationships within the family Phyllanthaceae.
